description_long: This pitchfork features a long, straight handle with a visually segmented pattern in shades of brown and orange, suggesting a wooden construction with possible painted or stained sections. The head of the pitchfork consists of three evenly spaced, rectangular tines, each with a blocky, pixelated appearance, giving it a stylized, almost digital look. The tines are attached to a horizontal bar that is perpendicular to the handle, with the outer tines extending slightly outward. The handle appears to be smooth and cylindrical, tapering slightly toward the end. There are no visible grips or caps at the end of the handle, and the overall design is simple and utilitarian. The coloration and finish suggest it is intended for decorative or stylized use rather than heavy-duty agricultural work.
description: A stylized pitchfork with a long, segmented wooden handle and three rectangular, blocky tines in shades of brown and orange.
description_view_0: Front view showing three evenly spaced, rectangular tines and the full length of the handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the tines and handle, with minimal width visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the three tines and handle from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and straight alignment of the handle and tines.
